case_title: JOSEPH K. BRAVO VS. GLORIA POUNCIL
cause_of_action: PETITION RE: ARBITRATION (PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD)

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER JOSEPH BRAVO'S PETITION TO CONFIRM AWARD. ARGUED. COURT HAVING REVIEWED THE PLEADINGS AND HAVING HEARD ORAL ARGUMENT RULES AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. THE AWARD OF $401,760 IS CONFIRMED. THE COURT DENIES MR. BRAVO'S REQUEST FOR $3,500. PAGE 8 OF THE AWARD INDICATES THAT SUM IS MR. BRAVO'S DEBT AND THERE IS NO ADDUCED BASIS TO SHIFT THE DEBT TO MS. POUNCIL. MR. BRAVO MAY RECOVER $450 FOR FILING THE INSTANT PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD PURSUANT TO BUSINESS & PROFESSIONS CODE 6203(C). ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: W. TRUPEK, COURT REPORTER: LORETTA NAJERA, CSR# 5153, EMAIL: LNAJERA@SFTC.ORG

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Petition And Petition To Confirm Award
Off Calendar Apr-22-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Joe K. Bravo, Esq., Bravo & Margulies, 415-512-6700, appearing on his own behalf as pro per plaintiff
Gloria Pouncil, 415-424-7143, appearing on her own behalf as pro per Defendant

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER JOSEPH BRAVO's Petition To Confirm Award.

Petitioner Joseph Bravo's petition to confirm arbitration award is granted. The award of $401,760 is confirmed. The court denies Mr. Bravo's request for $3,500. Page 8 of the award indicates that sum is Mr. Bravo's debt and there is no adduced basis to shift the debt to Ms. Pouncil. Mr. Bravo may recover $450 for filing the instant petition to confirm arbitration award pursuant to Business & Professions Code 6203(c).

Order and Judgment signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Loretta Najera, CSR# 5153, email: lnajera@sftc.org, Reported. =(302/EPS)

MOTION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD
